subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

files.fileName: khl893_Final IA Draft Math - Google Docs.pdf

strengths: Well-structured with a clear introduction, body and conclusion
Coherent logical development in linking sections (data â modelling â validity tests â evaluation)
Relevant use of figures and tables placed appropriately within the text
weaknesses: Inconsistent table formatting and duplicated headings disrupt seamless flow
Verbose repetition of definitions (e.g., RMSE, transition probability)
Occasional tangential socio-economic commentary that is not directly relevant
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are formatting glitches and some repetition, the exploration is highly coherent, well organised and largely concise.  The clear progression and effective placement of visual elements elevate it to the top band for presentation.

strengths: Consistent use of standard mathematical symbols and notation throughout
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatter plots with regression lines, Markov-chain diagram)
Clear explanations of most mathematical steps
weaknesses: Occasional notation inconsistencies (e.g., lowercase vs uppercase RÂ², unit omissions)
Axis labels and scaling choices are sometimes ambiguous
Minor labeling errors (e.g., ânumber of candidates (y)â)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs sophisticated combinations of algebraic expressions and visual representations, with overall clear and consistent mathematical language.  Minor slips do not detract from the highly effective communication, meriting the top band.

strengths: Clear personal anecdote about observing RTAs in Mathare showing authentic engagement
Significant initiative in collecting local data and comparing two distinct models
weaknesses: Markov-chain implementation remains superficial with flawed transition probabilities
Personal approach, while visible, does not permeate every stage of the exploration
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, but it lacks the depth and consistently outstanding creativity needed for the highest band.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on RMSE and interpretation of RÂ²
Critique of model assumptions (linearity, data intensity of Markov chain)
Clear linkage of reflection to enhanced understanding of modelling accuracy
weaknesses: Reflection is not deeply critical or systematic in addressing calculation discrepancies
Limited exploration of how findings might reshape the studentâs theoretical understanding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student offers meaningful analysis and clear connections to mathematical understanding, but lacks the depth and critical rigour required for the top band.

strengths: Appropriate application of linear regression, RMSE, RÂ² and first-order Markov process within the course scope
Mathematics is correct in the main with only minor errors
Calculations demonstrate a strong grasp of HL-level techniques
weaknesses: Transition matrix contains an impossible negative probability and rows do not sum to one
Variable definitions (independent vs dependent) are occasionally mixed up
Unit-scaling choices are not always made explicit
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical methods are largely accurate and used effectively at a high level.  Minor computational errors and clarity issues prevent full sophistication, placing it solidly in the second-highest band for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personally relevant Kenyan context, collecting data, and deciding to compare two distinct models indicate significant independent thinking.  However, the mathematical approaches themselves are standard syllabus techniques, so evidence is not outstanding.

The student shows good conceptual grasp of regression diagnostics and basic Markov ideas, but misconceptions appear (e.g., stating y is independent, mishandling transition probabilities).  Understanding is good in parts rather than thorough or rigorous, fitting band 3.

Most numerical work (gradient, RMSE, rÂ²) is calculated correctly, yet the transition matrix contains an impossible negative probability and rows do not sum to 1, and unit scaling is unclear.  Errors are present but not pervasive, giving âmostly correctâ mathematics.

Most paragraphs and calculations are directly tied to the research question, but there is noticeable repetition (e.g., repeated definitions of RMSE and repeated explanations of transition probability).  The writing is verbose in places and contains tangential socio-economic commentary.  This matches âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

Standard mathematical symbols (y = mx + c, RMSE, rÂ², transition matrices) are used correctly most of the time and variables are generally defined.  Inconsistencies such as calling y both independent and dependent and occasional unit omissions show it is not fully consistent, but usage is âmostly appropriateâ throughout.

Explanations of steps are generally clear, but notation slips (e.g., switching between percentages and raw numbers, unexplained negative probability in the matrix) create inconsistency.  Thus communication is âmostly clear with some consistencyâ, fitting band 2.

Linear regression with RMSE/rÂ² and a first-order Markov process are fully within HL AI scope and applied appropriately to the dataset; both are directly relevant to the aim.  The mathematics is used purposefully, but remains routine rather than sophisticated, aligning with âused effectivelyâ (band 4).

The student employs tables, two scatter plots with regression lines, and a Markov-chain diagram in addition to algebraic expressions.  These representations genuinely aid interpretation, so the use is effective, though not sophisticated or dynamic enough for the top band.
The student reflects on RMSE magnitude, interprets rÂ², and lists strengths and weaknesses of each model.  This constitutes meaningful but not exhaustive reflection; depth is adequate for band 2.

Combining regression with a (somewhat novel) Markov-chain attempt demonstrates creativity and initiative beyond a single-model study, yet the Markov implementation is superficial and the transition matrix is flawed, so creativity is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstanding.â

The exploration follows a recognisable progression (introduction â data â modelling â validity tests â evaluation).  Sections are linked logically and the reader can follow the investigative thread without getting lost.  Nevertheless, the narrative is occasionally interrupted by layout glitches (duplicated headings, long digressions about NTSA policy) and the logic between the linear model and the Markov model is not tightly connected.  These shortcomings prevent the work from reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor but still demonstrate clear logical development.

The evaluation section critiques linearity assumptions and data-intensity of Markov chains, showing some critique.  Analysis is present but not âdeepâ or systematically referenced to mathematical evidence, placing it in the âmeaningfulâ band.

Reflection consistently links back to the studentâs understanding of modelling accuracy and policy implications, demonstrating a clear connection to mathematical understanding.  However, the enhancement of understanding is not explored in depth, suiting band 2.

A contents page, labelled sections, and a final evaluation give the study a solid structure.  Figures and tables are inserted in the correct places and referenced in the text.  However, table formatting is erratic and some captions are confusing, so the flow is not completely seamless.  This aligns with the âwell-organisedâ rather than the âexceptionally well-organisedâ description.

Personal anecdotes about Mathare and discussion of how the findings might affect the studentâs travel habits show a clear personal approach driving the work, but this does not permeate every stage deeply enough for the highest band.

annotations.comment: Notation for the coefficient of determination fluctuates between lowercase $$r^2$$ and uppercase. Use consistent notation (e.g., $$R^2$$) throughout.
Table III is overly complex with merged cells and unclear headings. Simplify the column structure for better readability.
The transition matrix rows do not sum to 1, invalidating it as a Markov matrix. Normalize each row so total probability equals 1.
The table of contents exhibits inconsistent formatting and duplicated headings, which disrupts the logical flow. Consider standardizing entry styles and ensuring each section is clearly indexed.
It would improve readability to include consistent page numbers alongside each TOC entry and align column widths. This will help the reader navigate more efficiently.

The description of NTSA data (âover 3000 fatalities with over 4000 fatalities not recordedâ) is unclear. The student should clarify how âunrecordedâ figures were estimated and cite the method or assumptions used.
The personal anecdote about observing RTAs in Mathare effectively demonstrates the studentâs authentic engagement and motivation in choosing this topic.
The plan to âinfer equations to calculate the total number of accidentsâ is vague. Specify the variables, notation and formula structure that will be used to derive these equations.
The methodology bullet on using probability to estimate expected victims lacks detail on the chosen distribution and assumptions. The student should define the probabilistic model explicitly.
Table 1âs headers are misaligned and duplicated, making it hard to match each column with its data. Reformat the header row so that headings clearly correspond to the data below.
The notation â¯2 over the number 2 appears to be an OCR artifact. Ensure numerical labels on the table are presented correctly and consistently.
The scatter plotâs x-axis label (âyears after 2009â) is ambiguous and differs from the axis scale in the table. Align axis labels and units to your data representation.
The introductory description of the scatter plot repeats the date range and assumes axis units without explanation. Be concise and define all axes clearly.
The text mislabels y as the independent variable and x also as independent. In regression, y should be the dependent variable. Correct variable definitions.
The calculation of gradient uses scaled points (Ã10Â²) but does not clarify this scaling choice. Explicitly state any unit conversion or scaling applied to y-values.
The narrative repeats the regression formula multiple times. Streamline the exposition by defining $$y=mx+c$$ once and then referencing it.
After obtaining differing manual and GDC regression models, the student should reflect on possible calculation or rounding errors and discuss the discrepancy critically.
Claiming that the red model âbest fitsâ based on closeness alone is subjective. Quantify this choice using residuals or RMSE comparisons.
The phrase ânumber of candidates(y)â appears to be a placeholder error. Use precise terminology such as âpredicted fatalities $$\, othat{y_i}$$â.
The RMSE definition paragraph is lengthy and repeats earlier points. Summarize the formula concisely and refer to it in subsequent calculations.
The residual is computed as predicted minus actual, but the formula uses $$y_i - \,\widehat{y_i}$$. Reconcile the subtraction order to match the definition.
Squared residuals are rounded inconsistently to four significant figures. Apply uniform rounding rules throughout to maintain accuracy.
The RMSE table should explicitly note the unit scaling (Ã10Â²) applied to fatalities so readers can interpret residual magnitudes correctly.
In the $$r^2$$ calculation, ârssâ and âRSSâ appear interchangeable. Adopt consistent capitalization for residual sums.
The transition probability table includes a negative value (â0.062), which is impossible. Recheck calculations and ensure probabilities stay within [0,1].
